perturbation.original_text: At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party or destroy all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files), provided, however, that, the receiving party or its Representatives shall not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Evaluation Material held electronically in archive or backup systems in accordance with general systems archiving and backup policies.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files). Destruction of Evaluation Material is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that destruction of Evaluation Material is strictly prohibited, while the original clause allows for either delivery or destruction of the material upon request. This creates uncertainty about what the receiving party is obligated to do with the Evaluation Material upon request and introducing an in-text contradiction with the exception clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Evaluation Material shall remain the property of the disclosing party and the receiving party agrees not to assert any claim of title or ownership in the disclosing party’s Evaluation Material or any portion thereof. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Transaction, such party shall promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. At any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party and its Representatives shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party all Evaluation Material (and all copies, reproductions thereof) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files). Destruction of Evaluation Material is strictly prohibited. Upon the request of the disclosing party, a duly authorized representative of the receiving party shall certify to the disclosing party that the receiving party and its Representatives have complied with this paragraph (6). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of all Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives shall continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Destruction of Evaluation Material is strictly prohibited', which directly contradicts the original clause that allowed for either delivery or destruction of the material upon request. The contradicted text includes this conflicting statement, creating a direct in-text contradiction and making it unclear whether the receiving party should deliver or is prohibited from destroying the Evaluation Material, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered, and until such time, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that a binding agreement regarding a Transaction may be formed through mutual conduct and verbal agreement, even without a fully executed definitive agreement. By engaging in discussions and exchanging Evaluation Material, both parties acknowledge the intent to be legally bound.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that a binding agreement can be formed through conduct and verbal agreement, contradicting the original clause that requires a final definitive agreement for any legal obligation to arise. This undermines the intended protection against informal agreements and introduces significant legal uncertainty about the formation of a contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that a binding agreement regarding a Transaction may be formed through mutual conduct and verbal agreement, even without a fully executed definitive agreement. By engaging in discussions and exchanging Evaluation Material, both parties acknowledge the intent to be legally bound. Each party reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material to the other party or its Representatives under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction between the parties, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that a binding agreement can be formed through mutual conduct and verbal agreement, which directly contradicts the original text's requirement of a final definitive agreement for any legal obligation. The contradicted text repeats the changed text. This creates a clear inconsistency within the contract regarding the formation of a binding agreement, aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of five percent (5%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership;
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that, for a period of six (6) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of ten percent (10%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership;
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction by changing both the duration of the standstill period from twelve months to six months and the percentage of beneficial ownership from five percent to ten percent. This creates confusion about the actual restrictions imposed on EMC Corporation, making it unclear for how long and at what ownership threshold the standstill provision applies. This in-text contradiction causes uncertainty about the extent of the standstill obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree that, for a period of six (6) months from the date of this Agreement, unless you receive the prior authorized approval of an authorized Isilon officer or director, you will not directly or indirectly (including, without limitation, by assisting or forming a group (a “ l3D Group ”) within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (as amended, the “ Exchange Act ”)): (a) acquire or offer to acquire, seek, propose or agree to acquire, by means of a repurchase, tender or exchange offer, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of ten percent (10%) or more of any securities or assets of Isilon (including any securities or assets of Isilon that you or any of your controlled affiliates already, directly or indirectly, own) including, without limitation, rights or options to acquire such ownership; (b) seek or propose to influence, advise, change or control the management, Board of Directors, governance or certificate of incorporation or bylaws, including, without limitation, by means of a solicitation of proxies (as such terms are defined in Rule l4a-l of Regulation l4A promulgated pursuant to Section 14 of the Exchange Act, disregarding clause (iv) of Rule l4a-l(l)(2) and including any otherwise exempt solicitation pursuant to Rule l4a-2(b)) or participating in any election contest or seeking to influence, advise or direct the vote of any holder of securities of Isilon; (c) offer, seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Isilon or any of its subsidiaries or businesses; (d) make any request to amend or waive this provision or any other provision of this paragraph (9); (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action which would reasonably be expected to require Isilon to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement; or (f) enter into any discussions (excluding discussions with your Representatives), arrangements, understanding or agreement with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, including, without limitation, forming, joining or otherwise participating in any l3D Group in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original standstill agreement by altering both the duration (from 12 months to 6 months) and the ownership threshold (from 5% to 10%). The contradicted text also states that the duration is six months and the ownership threshold is ten percent, suggesting both the changed and contradicted text are located in the same location. This implies that the original text contradicted the contradicted text. This change creates a clear in-text contradiction because the same contractual provision now reflects different terms at different locations, leading to confusion and uncertainty about the actual obligations. The altered percentages and durations conflict and create inconsistency about which terms should be enforced. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement, each party agrees that it will not, directly or indirectly (including through its Representatives), solicit for employment any employee of the other party or its subsidiaries who such party comes in contact with, or is introduced to, or is discussed as a key employee, in connection with a Transaction; provided , however , that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prohibit (x) general solicitations of employment conducted through customary means and not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, or (y) the employment of any person who contacts a party on his or her own initiative without any violation of the foregoing provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it is encouraged to solicit for employment any employee of the other party or its subsidiaries, provided there is no prior agreement between the employee and their current employer restricting such solicitations.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by completely reversing the non-solicitation clause. Instead of prohibiting solicitation of employees, it encourages it, subject to a condition. This creates a direct conflict with the original intent and places the parties in an uncertain position regarding employee recruitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it is encouraged to solicit for employment any employee of the other party or its subsidiaries, provided there is no prior agreement between the employee and their current employer restricting such solicitations. (11) Securities Laws . Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited solicitation of employees, while the changed text encourages it, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency. The explanation confirms this reversal constitutes an in-text contradiction. The contradictory text repeats the changed text, indicating both texts are actively contradicting the non-solicitation clause and instead allowing the solicitation.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years from the date first written above.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year from the date first written above, with automatic renewal for successive one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the current term.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the originally stated term of the agreement. Instead of a fixed five-year term, the agreement now has a one-year term with automatic renewal. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and the conditions for termination, introducing an in-text contradiction about the term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby represents that it has the power and authority (including corporate power and corporate authority, if applicable) to execute and deliver this Agreement and that this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding agreement of such party, enforceable in accordance with its terms. The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year from the date first written above, with automatic renewal for successive one-year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the current term. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts. The exchange of signature pages to this Agreement (in counterparts or otherwise) by facsimile transmission or other electronic transmission shall be sufficient to bind the parties to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed five-year term, but the changed text establishes a one-year term with automatic renewal, which is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text contains the changed text; this does not mean that the changed text has not introduced a contradiction. The changed text modifies the term of the agreement from a fixed term to a renewable term, triggering an inconsistency within the document regarding the duration of the agreement, therefore a clear contradiction exists. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections (the original agreement and the changed section) now provide conflicting information about the term of the agreement.
